ab: Mtduwa ku smiling sunan hug?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Tduwa, smruwa ku bi.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: Mtduwa ku smiling sunan?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: Aji ku, aji lala ka gklaan mu ka yaku.
en: B:Don't, I don't know much about it.

ab: Niqan jiyax su babaw kndaxan hug? Kmusa ku bi qmita sunan?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: Iq, mtaga ku sapah mu sunan.
en: B: Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

ab: Niqan jiyax su saman hug? kmpsiling ku quri endaan Truku sbiyaw.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於太魯閣族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I would like to ask you about the legend of the Truku people.

ab: Mha ku daya ka saman, aji ku mniq sapah.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

ab: Qnbahangan mu o mkla su bi quri knkla rudan sbiyaw ka isu,kmpsiling ku bi sunan.
ch: A:我聽說您對阿美族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very familiar with the traditional customs of the Amis, so I would like to ask you a few questions. *Please change [Amis] to your ethnic group.

ab: Aji lala ka klaan mu, kiya ka kiya ni ququ ku msiyuk wah.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don't know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: Mtduwa ku muda mangakl sasaw utux hug? kika mha lxan bi klaan hici.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: Iq o, tduwa.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: Piya hngkwasan su ka hkawas sayang?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: Mngari kmxalan maspat ku da.
en: B: I'm 98 years old.

ab: Embbiyax su bi na.
en: A: You're so young.

ab: Ini wa! rudan ku da.
en: B: No, I'm not! I'm old.

ab: Pnaah su inu hug?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: Pnaah ku alang Dumung.
en: B: I'm from Dumung.

ab: Seejiq Bunun ka kuyuh su hug?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是布農族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also a Bunun?

ab: Aji, seejiq Embgala ka hiya.
en: B:No, she's a Thayan.

ab: Mnmanu klgan kari klaan su rmngaw ka isu?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: Mkla ku rmngaw kari Rukay ni kari Paywang ka yaku.
ch: B: 我會說魯凱語和排灣語。
en: B: I speak Rukai and Paiwan.

ab: Qrasun mu balay mtduwa ku smiling sunan ka sayang,rngaw cici han,quri pndakar tmsamat ga?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It's a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: Iq wa, embiyax ku siida o musa ku tmsmat dgiyaq kdjiyax ka yaku.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: Mhuway su bi, Tayal bi knmalu ka tnisa su sayang.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: Ini bi qnuqu ka rnngaw mu, hnici snruan rudan sbiyaw.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: Bitaq hini ka snlingan mu sunan, mqaras ku balay ka pnseasu su kari.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That's all for today's interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: Iya qsiqa, pprngaw ta duri.
en: B: You're welcome, let's talk again.

ab: Mowsa ku da, smulu ku qmbahang lala bi endaan su sbiyaw duri.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I'm going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: Iq wa, knhuway musa.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
